other_benefits: Global internet health network; no relocation required

other_benefits: Princeton NJ in-person; tech policy research community

other_benefits: Residential program at University of Pennsylvania

flags: Verify current cycle dates; most recent confirmed cycle was 2024
notes: US-based Penn residential program (distinct from Oxford UK posting)

name: Graduate Research Fellowship Program (GRFP)
other_benefits: Portable; use at any accredited US institution; prestige
eligibility: US citizens, nationals, or permanent residents; STEM including STEM education
flags: STEM and STEM education fields only
notes: One of the most prestigious and portable US graduate fellowships

name: American Dissertation Fellowship
other_benefits: Flexible use: tuition, living, childcare, research
eligibility: Must identify as a woman; US citizen or permanent resident; enrolled at accredited institution; first doctoral degree only; final dissertation year
flags: Women only; final dissertation year only; first doctoral degree only
notes: Oldest non-institutional graduate funding source for women in the US

name: Mellon/ACLS Dissertation Innovation Fellowship
other_benefits: Mentorship; humanities and interpretive social sciences network
eligibility: US-eligible; CRITICAL EXCLUSIONS: EdD, education, public policy, public administration, law, STEM, social work, business not eligible; humanities and interpretive social sciences only
flags: EXCLUDES EdD, education, public policy, public admin, law, STEM, social work, business. Humanities and interpretive social sciences ONLY.
notes: Read exclusion list carefully before applying

organization: National Academy of Education / Spencer Foundation
name: NAEd/Spencer Dissertation Fellowship
amount: $27,500 for up to 2 years of dissertation writing
other_benefits: Professional development retreats; NAEd mentorship
eligibility: Enrolled at US graduate school; US citizenship NOT required (includes undocumented); dissertation must focus on education in any discipline
flags: Dissertation must focus on education (broadly defined)
notes: 35 awards/year; one of the most prestigious education research dissertation fellowships

name: ABF Doctoral Fellowship in Law and Inequality
other_benefits: Chicago-based; sociolegal research community; AccessLex variant may offer up to $48,000
eligibility: US citizens, nationals, or permanent residents; ABD social-science or sociolegal doctoral candidates; in-residence Chicago
flags: Social science and sociolegal research focus; in-residence Chicago required
notes: Formerly ABF-LSA-NSF; verify current cycle and amount on ABF website

name: Kauffman Knowledge Challenge
other_benefits: Entrepreneurship research network
eligibility: US-eligible; doctoral students; entrepreneurship research focus
flags: Entrepreneurship and innovation research focus; biannual cycle
notes: Note: standalone Kauffman Dissertation Fellowship discontinued 2018; this is the current grant program

name: RSF Dissertation Research Grants
other_benefits: Social science research network
eligibility: Enrolled ABD doctoral students at US institutions; US citizenship not required
flags: Social science research focus
notes: Supports dissertation research in the social sciences

name: Horowitz Foundation Research Grant
amount: $10,000 total ($7,500 + $2,500 completion) + special awards up to +$5,000
other_benefits: Social policy research recognition
eligibility: Current PhD or DrPH candidates (degree not yet received); US citizenship not required; social policy research
flags: Must not have received doctorate by application date
notes: Social policy dissertation research focus; all social science disciplines

name: Summer Associate Program (GSAP)
other_benefits: Policy research mentorship; RAND network
eligibility: Must reside in US; full-time doctoral or professional students (PhD, EdD, and equivalent); not undergrad or postdoc
flags: EdD eligible; must reside in US during program
notes: Summer policy research fellowship at RAND; broad policy fields

name: CAST Technology and Security Policy Fellowship
other_benefits: US and UK placements; security clearance pathway
eligibility: US and UK citizens/work auth; security clearance required or anticipated; open to graduate students and researchers
flags: Security clearance required; US and UK only
notes: AI safety and technology policy; rolling admissions

name: Weinstein JAMS International Fellowship
other_benefits: ADR and dispute resolution professional network
eligibility: US-eligible; practitioners and researchers in alternative dispute resolution (ADR); not exclusively students
flags: ADR field focus; open to practitioners, not exclusively graduate students
notes: Alternative dispute resolution research and practice focus

name: National Defense Science and Engineering Graduate (NDSEG) Fellowship
amount: $43,200/year ($3,600/month) + full tuition and fees + up to $1,600 health insurance; 3 years
other_benefits: DoD national labs access; STEM leadership network
eligibility: US citizens or nationals ONLY (permanent residents NOT eligible; dual citizens OK); first 2 years of graduate study in STEM/engineering; 3+ years remaining
flags: US citizens and nationals ONLY (no permanent residents); STEM and engineering fields only; early-stage graduate students only
notes: Cannot be held simultaneously with NSF GRFP or other major fellowships

name: Paul and Daisy Soros Fellowship for New Americans
amount: Up to $90,000 total: $25,000/year stipend (2 years) + 50% tuition up to $20,000/year
other_benefits: New Americans community and network; Fall Conference paid
eligibility: Must be a New American: US citizen (naturalized or born to non-US-citizen parents), green card holder, asylee, refugee, or DACA recipient, OR graduated US high school and college; age 30 or younger; not past 3rd year of graduate program; all fields
flags: New Americans ONLY (immigrants or children of immigrants meeting specific criteria); age 30 or younger; not past 3rd year of grad program
notes: For the 2027 Fellowship; any graduate or professional field at any US university

name: Hertz Fellowship
amount: Up to $250,000 over 5 years: ~$44,000/year 9-month stipend + full tuition equivalent + ~$5,000/year dependents allowance
other_benefits: Mentorship; Hertz Fellows network; coordinates with other fellowships to maximize benefit
eligibility: US citizens or permanent residents; college seniors, first-year graduate students, or gap-year applicants; PhD in applied physical/biological sciences, math, or engineering at a US institution; no professional degrees (MD, JD, MBA)
flags: STEM only: applied physical/biological sciences, math, engineering; PhD only; early-stage applicants only
notes: One of the most prestigious and generous US STEM graduate fellowships

name: AERA Minority Dissertation Fellowship in Education Research
amount: Up to $25,000 + invited AERA poster session + mentoring and career workshop
other_benefits: AERA Annual Meeting participation; senior scholar mentorship
eligibility: US citizens or permanent residents who are members of underrepresented racial/ethnic minority groups in higher education (African Americans, Alaskan Natives, American Indians, Asian Americans, Hispanics/Latinos, Native Hawaiians/Pacific Islanders); ABD at dissertation writing stage; education, social science, humanities, or related disciplines
flags: Underrepresented racial and ethnic minority scholars ONLY; education research focus; ABD dissertation writing stage
notes: Write-up fellowship; 1-year nonrenewable award beginning July

name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars Program
amount: Full funding up to 3 years: full tuition and fees + living stipend + annual travel stipend + one-time relocation stipend
other_benefits: King Global Leadership Program; Stanford network; multidisciplinary community
eligibility: Open to all nationalities including US; must apply to and be admitted to a full-time Stanford graduate program (MA, MS, MBA, JD, MD, MFA, PhD); bachelor's earned Jan 2019 or later (for 2027 cohort); all fields
flags: Requires concurrent admission to a Stanford graduate program; bachelor's degree recency requirement
notes: World's largest fully endowed university-wide graduate fellowship program; ~100 scholars per year

name: Harriett G. Jenkins Predoctoral Fellowship (JPFP)
amount: ~$27,000/year stipend (PhD) or ~$22,000 (Master's) + ~$8,500-$10,000 tuition offset + ~$8,000 for 10-week NASA Center research experience; up to 3 years
other_benefits: NASA Center research experience; STEM leadership network
eligibility: US citizens; STEM/NASA-related fields; master's and doctoral students; aimed at underrepresented groups (women, minorities, persons with disabilities)
flags: VERIFY current cycle is open at intern.nasa.gov (OSSI); federal program subject to funding availability; STEM fields; diversity-focused
notes: Apply through NASA One Stop Shopping Initiative (OSSI) at intern.nasa.gov; select Fellowship type

name: P.E.O. Scholar Awards
other_benefits: P.E.O. Sisterhood network
eligibility: Must identify as a woman; US or Canada citizen or legal permanent resident; enrolled in doctoral program at accredited US or Canadian institution; within 2 years of graduation with at least 1 full academic year remaining; all fields
flags: Women only; must be NOMINATED by a local P.E.O. chapter (contact your nearest chapter to initiate); within 2 years of graduation
notes: Nomination window Aug 20-Nov 20; contact a local P.E.O. Sisterhood chapter to be nominated before applying

name: Charlotte W. Newcombe Doctoral Dissertation Fellowship
amount: $31,000 for 12 months of final-year dissertation writing; graduate schools asked to waive tuition and fees
other_benefits: Tuition waiver request to institution; health insurance maintained
eligibility: PhD candidates at accredited US graduate schools in humanities or social sciences in the final dissertation writing stage; US citizenship not required; EdD, JD, PsyD, DMin and other professional degrees NOT eligible; STEM only if religion/ethics/values are central
flags: EXCLUDES EdD and other professional degrees; topic MUST centrally address religion, ethics, morals, or values; humanities and social sciences only
notes: Nation's largest award for PhD candidates addressing religion, ethics, or values; use Program Key CWNF to apply

name: GEM Fellowship
amount: Full tuition and fees at GEM member university + $16,000 first-year stipend + continued departmental funding through year 5 (PhD) + at least 1 paid summer internship with a GEM employer
other_benefits: Paid employer internship; GEM employer and university network
eligibility: US citizens or permanent residents; underrepresented minority students (Black, Hispanic, Native American) in STEM; master's and doctoral students in applied science and engineering; must apply to at least 3 GEM member universities
flags: Underrepresented minority students ONLY; STEM and engineering fields only; must apply to 3+ GEM member universities simultaneously
notes: Presidential Award-winning program; 5,000+ STEM leaders trained; strong full-funding package

name: Centennial Center Research Grants
deadline: Summer cycle: verify on apsanet.org (Spring Mar 15 cycle closed)
amount: Up to $2,500 (individual grant; APSA offers $40,000+ total annually)
other_benefits: APSA professional network; political science research visibility
eligibility: APSA members; graduate students at any stage from any institution welcome; priority to those with limited departmental resources; APSA membership required throughout grant period
flags: Small award: up to $2,500, research costs only (not a living stipend); APSA membership required; cannot have received Centennial Center funding in prior 4 years
notes: Research costs only: data collection, software, travel to archives; not for living expenses. Spring cycle closed; summer cycle dates vary - verify on website.

name: Smithsonian Institution Fellowship Program (Predoctoral)
other_benefits: In-residence at Smithsonian facilities; collections and research infrastructure access
eligibility: PhD candidates conducting dissertation research; university must certify all requirements except dissertation are met before start; in-residence at Smithsonian (3-12 months); all nationalities; US-based study
flags: In-residence at Smithsonian required; research must align with Smithsonian collections and research areas; doctoral candidate stage
notes: Broad disciplinary range; research must connect to Smithsonian research interests

name: Gilliam Fellowship for Advanced Study
amount: ~$50,000/year for up to 3 years (stipend + discretionary + institutional tuition/fees allowance + community engagement allowance)
other_benefits: Advisor mentorship development course; Gilliam Annual Meeting; HHMI Science Meetings
eligibility: 2nd or 3rd-year PhD students with 2+ years remaining in biological or biomedical PhD or dual-degree program at an HHMI-eligible US institution; US citizens, permanent residents, DACA, or international students eligible to receive funding at their institution
flags: Biological and biomedical PhD ONLY; must apply jointly as an advisor-student pair; diversity and inclusion focused; CANNOT be held concurrently with NSF GRFP or NIH F31; verify open vs nomination-based at your institution
notes: Application opens Sept 1 via HHMI ARC system; current HHMI page says open competition (no nomination), but verify with your institution

name: Selected Professions Fellowship
other_benefits: AAUW professional network
eligibility: Must identify as a woman; US citizen or permanent resident; pursuing FIRST full-time master's or professional degree in designated STEM/underrepresented fields (engineering, computer science, architecture, math/statistics, select medical programs); no prior graduate degree
flags: Women only; STEM and designated professional fields ONLY; first graduate degree only (no prior master's or doctoral degree)
notes: Fellowship year July 1 to June 30; fields include architecture, CS/AI, engineering, math, physical sciences

name: Computational Science Graduate Fellowship (CSGF)
amount: $45,000/year stipend + full tuition and fees + $5,000 first-year academic allowance ($1,000/renewed year); up to 4 years
other_benefits: 12-week practicum at a DOE national lab; annual DC program review; interdisciplinary STEM community
eligibility: US citizens or lawful permanent residents; full-time PhD study at an accredited US university; engineering, physical, computer, mathematical, or life sciences using high-performance computing; college seniors, first-year grad students, or matriculating students
flags: Computational science and HPC focus; engineering and sciences only; early-stage applicants
notes: Established 1991; 675+ fellows trained; strong HPC and DOE national lab connection

name: Ruth L. Kirschstein NRSA Individual Predoctoral Fellowship (F31)
amount: Predoctoral stipend (single level, set annually by NIH; ~$28,000+ in recent years) + 60% of tuition up to $16,000/year + institutional allowance for health, supplies, books, travel; up to 5 years
other_benefits: NIH institute affiliation; prestige; mentored research training
eligibility: US citizens or permanent residents; enrolled in research doctoral program (PhD, EdD, DrPH, ScD, and equivalent) in biomedical, behavioral, or clinical health-related fields at a US institution; must identify a faculty sponsor before applying
flags: Biomedical, behavioral, and clinical health-related research fields only; faculty sponsor required; cannot be held with another federal fellowship providing stipend
notes: Current announcement PA-25-422; FY2026 stipend levels confirmed; check NIH for current stipend rate

organization: Josephine de Karman Fellowship Trust / Pasadena Community Foundation
name: Josephine de Karman Fellowship
amount: $28,000 for one academic year (fall and spring); 4 awards per cycle
other_benefits: Dissertation completion support; humanities priority consideration
eligibility: Final-year PhD candidates defending in or around June 2028; currently enrolled at a specific list of California universities ONLY (Caltech, Stanford, UC Berkeley, UC Davis, UC Irvine, UCLA, UC Riverside, UC San Diego, UC Santa Barbara, UC Santa Cruz, USC); all disciplines including international students; no undergrads, master's, JD, or postdocs
flags: CURRENTLY RESTRICTED to PhD students at listed California universities ONLY; verify your institution is on the eligible list; final dissertation year only; special consideration for humanities
notes: Under new Pasadena Community Foundation stewardship since 2023; eligibility narrowed to California institutions

name: ASA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grants (ASA DDRIG)
other_benefits: ASA professional network; political science and social science research visibility
eligibility: Doctoral student enrolled at a PhD-granting institution physically located in the US; completed pre-dissertation requirements; conducting empirical dissertation research in any sociological subfield; must have a faculty sponsor; may not have previously received any NSF-funded DDRIG
flags: Sociological research focus; research-cost grant (not a living stipend); faculty sponsor required; prior NSF-funded DDRIG recipients not eligible; 2027 cycle contingent on continued NSF funding
notes: NSF-funded through ASA; program funded through 2028; open to any sociological subfield regardless of method

name: Wenner-Gren Dissertation Fieldwork Grant
other_benefits: Covers travel, living expenses during fieldwork, research assistance, software, and other direct research costs
eligibility: Doctoral students enrolled in anthropology or related fields conducting dissertation fieldwork; all nationalities; must have a dissertation supervisor at same institution; all requirements except dissertation must be met before start date
flags: Anthropology and related fields only; all nationalities eligible (not US-restricted); research-cost grant, not a living stipend
notes: Two cycles/year: May 1 and Nov 1. No preference for methodology, location, topic, or subfield. Review takes 6 months.

name: Pallas Foundation Fellowship
amount: Paid; verify stipend amount on website
other_benefits: Washington DC placement; access to senior DoD, State Department, NSC, and Intelligence Community officials; professional development programming; career guidance and job placement support
eligibility: Graduate students, recent graduates, and current seniors; interest in national security, defense, AI, cyber, and technology policy; DC-based (hybrid format)
flags: Deadline July 10, 2026 (urgent); paid amount not publicly listed; verify compensation on website; consulting-placement format
notes: Fall 2026 cohort. Fellows support operational and research functions at Pallas Advisors consultancy. Weekly professional development programming included.

organization: American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS)
name: AAAS Mass Media Science and Engineering Fellowship
other_benefits: Placement at major US news outlet; science communication training; AAAS professional network
eligibility: Advanced undergraduate and graduate students in natural sciences, social sciences, or engineering at US institutions; US-eligible; all disciplines
flags: Summer placement at a news organization; science communication and media focus; not a research fellowship
notes: Places fellows at major newspapers, magazines, TV, and radio stations to report on science and policy. Strong for science-policy communication careers.

name: Truman Scholarship
other_benefits: Truman Scholar community; public service career development; priority consideration at select graduate programs
eligibility: US citizens; junior-year undergraduates or recent graduates planning graduate study in public service fields; must be nominated by institution; intent to pursue public service career
flags: Primarily for current juniors or recent undergrads (not current graduate students); institution nomination required; public service career intent required
notes: One of the most prestigious US public service scholarships. Supports graduate study in preparation for careers in government, nonprofit, or public service.

name: Harry Frank Guggenheim Emerging Scholar Award
other_benefits: HFG research network; access to foundation scholars working on violence and aggression
eligibility: Doctoral candidates writing dissertations on violence, aggression, and dominance (biological, social, cultural, psychological lenses); all nationalities; enrolled at institution anywhere in world; US-eligible
flags: Research must address violence, aggression, or dominance as central theme; dissertation stage only; competitive (approximately 10 awards/year)
notes: Supports dissertation research examining causes and control of violence, aggression, and dominance. Broad disciplinary scope including sociology, psychology, political science, anthropology, criminology.
